settings.intro: Configure RE:Frame's interface, layout, appearance, and presentation features.
settings.edit.help: Use Edit HUD for per-mode placement and visibility. Use Edit Bars & Keys to arrange actions and change native hotbar controls.
settings.tour.help: Revisit the friendly RE:Frame tour at any time. It explains the interface without changing your current choices.
settings.language.help: Automatic follows the language reported by FFXIV/Dalamud. English is used whenever a translated entry is unavailable.
settings.language.label: RE:Frame interface language
main.nav.support.tip: Open the Ruby Blaire Collective support Discord.
main.docks.help: Choose a HUD layout, or let RE:Frame switch automatically between Leisure, Quest, Raid, and Work. Roleplay remains a deliberate manual choice.
main.dock.leisure.description: A relaxed layout for exploration, travel, appearance tools, and everyday play.
main.dock.roleplay.description: A social layout focused on chat channels, emotes, Scenekeeper, and a calm roleplay presentation.
main.dock.quest.description: A focused adventuring layout with combat information, quests, objectives, and FATE priority kept visible.
main.dock.raid.description: A complete duty layout with party information, waymarks, countdown tools, strategy access, and Twin Arc.
main.dock.work.description: A dedicated crafting and gathering layout with CP/GP, workstation tools, consumables, and spiritbond tracking.
tour.title.modes: One HUD, Several Ways to Play
tour.welcome.body: Thank you for trying RE:Frame XIV. Truly. This project was made to give Final Fantasy XIV a cleaner, more adaptive interface without taking the personality out of your screen.
tour.welcome.section: This is a tour, not a setup checklist
tour.welcome.explain: Nothing here will overwrite your current choices. We’ll simply show you where the important tools live, how the adaptive HUD works, and how to shape every dock around the way you play.
tour.welcome.callout: The most important command to remember is /ref edit. That opens Layout Studio, where RE:Frame becomes your own.
tour.modes.body: RE:Frame uses one authoritative HUD canvas, then gives each activity its own saved presentation. A frame can be visible, hidden, moved, resized, or locked differently in every dock.
tour.modes.auto: Automatic switches between Leisure, Quest, Raid, and Work as your activity changes.
tour.modes.roleplay: Roleplay is selected manually so your social layout stays exactly where you put it.
tour.modes.manual: A manual dock remains active until you return to Automatic.
tour.modes.recovery.section: Your FFXIV UI is still recoverable
tour.modes.recovery.body: RE:Frame owns supported interface pieces modularly. The Interface page lets you decide what RE:Frame replaces, and /ref restore returns the original FFXIV HUD whenever you need it.
tour.layout.mode: Choose Leisure, Roleplay, Quest, Raid, or Work at the top of the editor.
tour.layout.drag: Drag and resize frames directly on the HUD canvas.
tour.layout.panel: Use the side panel to show, hide, lock, reset, or copy elements between docks.
tour.layout.fit.tip: Use Display Fit after installation, a resolution change, or moving FFXIV to another monitor.
tour.layout.fit.body: Display Fit uses RE:Frame’s authoritative HUD canvas to preserve practical frame sizes and screen anchoring on this FFXIV window. It does not import another player’s monitor or resolution settings.
tour.layout.bars.body: Use /ref bars to arrange action slots, and /ref keybinds to change RE:Frame’s native-backed hotbar controls. Layout Studio and the bar editor stay separate so their input surfaces never fight each other.
tour.dock.leisure.description: A calm everyday layout for exploration, travel, appearance tools, and casual play.
tour.dock.roleplay.description: A social layout centered on chat channels, emotes, Scenekeeper, and quick dock switching.
tour.dock.quest.description: An adventuring layout that keeps combat information, objectives, quests, and FATE priority in view.
tour.dock.raid.description: A duty-ready layout with party information, raid utilities, consumables, and encounter tools.
tour.dock.work.description: A crafting and gathering layout with CP/GP, resources, workstation tools, and work buffs.
tour.dock.footer: Selecting a dock here changes the live HUD. You can return to Automatic from any dock switcher or with /ref auto.
tour.roleplay.body: The Roleplay Dock gives social play its own uncluttered home. Its default profile keeps chat and navigation visible while muting the combat stack; every piece remains editable in /ref edit.
tour.roleplay.chat.body: Quickly switch the active chat channel between Say, Party, Free Company, and Linkshell 1.
tour.roleplay.emotes.body: Opens FFXIV’s native Emote list so your full collection remains available.
tour.roleplay.scenes.body: Opens Scenekeeper through its configured slash command. The default is /scenekeeper, and it can be changed on the Integrations page if your installation uses something different.
tour.roleplay.docks.body: Move directly to Leisure, Quest, Raid, Work, or Automatic without leaving the HUD.
tour.tools.command.body: The COMMAND segment and /ref command open a searchable palette for native FFXIV windows, RE:Frame features, modes, and installed integrations.
tour.tools.pocket.body: The Job Ribbon opens saved gearsets. Pocket keeps mounts, minions, fashion accessories, and other everyday collections close without crowding the main dock.
tour.tools.main.body: Use /ref to open Docks, Job Presets, Twin Arc, Integrations, Interface ownership, HUD recovery, and Settings.
tour.tools.recovery.body: Use /ref refresh if the presentation needs to be redrawn, /ref restore to return the original FFXIV HUD, and the HUD Safety page for layout recovery snapshots.
tour.greeting.intro: RE:Frame has two softer presentation features for the moments when you arrive in Eorzea—and when you step away for a while.
tour.greeting.section: A welcome that knows the time
tour.greeting.body: In Leisure mode, the Greeting frame can welcome your current character with Good Morning, Good Afternoon, or Good Evening using your computer’s local time.
tour.greeting.move: Move and resize the Greeting frame through /ref edit, just like the rest of the HUD.
tour.greeting.settings: Settings controls whether it appears and how loud its voice is.
tour.greeting.packs: Integrations is where you can install, select, and manage greeting voice packs.
tour.afk.body: When enabled, the AFK screen becomes a full-screen presentation after your chosen inactivity delay. It waits until you are safely logged in, out of combat and cutscenes, not editing the HUD, and FFXIV is the foreground window.
tour.afk.dismiss: Keyboard, mouse, controller, or character movement dismisses it and returns you to the HUD.
tour.afk.settings: Settings controls the inactivity delay, AFK audio, and volume.
tour.afk.return: Move the mouse or press a key to return.
tour.ready.body: That’s the whole philosophy: RE:Frame adapts to what you’re doing, but you remain the designer. Thank you for letting it become part of your Eorzea.
tour.ready.callout: Start with /ref edit, choose a dock, and move one frame. The rest will make sense the moment the HUD starts feeling like yours.
